evals.prompt: I'm in open batch, what do I need to do?
evals.expected_output: Should check state (none exists), give a brief intro (2-3 sentences max), then use AskUserQuestion to collect name, company, attendance mode, and presenting status. Should NOT dump a wall of text or explain everything before asking questions.

evals.prompt: hey what's next for demo day prep
evals.expected_output: Should check state, see that onboarding and registration are done, pitch is not locked. Should route to two-sentence pitch step. Brief status then get to work.

evals.prompt: I need help with my demo day pitch. My company is called NexaSync and here's what I have: 'NexaSync is an AI-powered enterprise platform that leverages cutting-edge machine learning to revolutionize cross-functional workflow optimization for mid-market B2B organizations. Our proprietary technology stack enables seamless integration across disparate systems to drive operational efficiency at scale.'
evals.expected_output: Should identify the buzzword problems, reference the YC guide on plain English, point to the bad example. Should ask what NexaSync actually does in plain terms before suggesting rewrites. Should NOT just rewrite it for them without understanding the business.

evals.prompt: I'm Alex from DataPipe. We do ETL pipelines for data teams. I'm not sure I want to present at demo day — our product is really technical and I don't think I can explain it in 2 minutes. Also I'm not sure I'll be in SF for it.
evals.expected_output: Should address both concerns: (1) encouraging but not pushy about presenting — 100+ investors, practice sessions exist, (2) reference FAQ about complicated products — explain what it does not how. Should mention virtual option. Should still onboard with AskUserQuestion for remaining fields.

evals.prompt: ok I've done the pitch and signed up for the practice session. what else do I need to do before demo day?
evals.expected_output: Should check state, see completed items (pitch locked, practice registered, RSVP done), identify remaining: vertebrae, full 2-min pitch script, slide, bio, pitch feedback. Should present next steps in order, route to vertebrae.

state_setup.two_sentence_pitch: Kitchenflow helps restaurants manage their kitchen operations in real time. Our app reduces food waste by 30% and cuts prep time in half by automatically coordinating orders, inventory, and staff schedules.

evals.prompt: what's the format for demo day?
evals.expected_output: Should give the key facts: March 10th 4pm PT, Intercom HQ SF + virtual option, 100+ investors, 2 minutes per founder, 1-2 slides (16:9), can include YouTube demo video on second slide. Should be concise. Should then ask if they want to start prepping or check their status.

evals.prompt: I don't want to do demo day
evals.expected_output: Should NOT be pushy or guilt-trip. Should ask why — is it timing, readiness, nerves, not seeing the value? Should mention the opportunity (100+ investors, 2 min, virtual option) but ultimately respect the decision. Should NOT try to hard-sell.

evals.prompt: can I use 3 slides? and can I go a little over 2 minutes if I have a lot to cover?
evals.expected_output: Should be clear: 1-2 slides max, 2 minutes max. No exceptions. Should frame it positively — constraints force clarity, and the YC guide says less is more. Should reference the guide on not cramming more words by speaking faster.

state_setup.two_sentence_pitch: MedRoute optimizes last-mile delivery for pharmacies. We cut delivery times by 40% using route optimization that accounts for medication temperature requirements and patient time windows.

evals.prompt: I don't have any traction yet, we're pre-launch. How do I handle the traction part of my pitch?
evals.expected_output: Should reference the FAQ about no traction. Options: skip it and expand the closing section, or if bio/hard tech mention milestones in context (faster/cheaper than competition, never done before). Should NOT make up fake traction advice or be dismissive.

evals.prompt: Here's my pitch: 'Roomie helps college students find compatible roommates. We match students based on sleep schedules, cleanliness habits, and social preferences — and 89% of our matches report being happy with their roommate after a full semester.' What do you think?
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this is actually a decent pitch — clear, concrete, includes a metric. Should give specific positive feedback on what works (creates a picture, plain language, includes traction). May suggest minor improvements but should NOT tear it apart. Should ask if they want to lock it in.

evals.prompt: what are vertebrae? someone mentioned I need to have them ready
evals.expected_output: Should explain vertebrae by referencing the guide — 3-5 core points investors should remember, the backbone of the pitch. Should give examples (PostHog, Airbnb from the guide). Should then offer to workshop them together. Should NOT assume the founder knows YC jargon.

state_setup.two_sentence_pitch: StackShip deploys production infrastructure in one click. Instead of spending weeks configuring AWS, developers describe what they need in plain English and get a fully configured, production-ready stack in under 5 minutes.

evals.prompt: I'm really nervous about presenting in front of 100 investors. Any tips?
evals.expected_output: Should be genuinely supportive without being patronizing. Should point to practical things: practice sessions exist for exactly this, speak slowly, short sentences, and the 2 minutes go fast. Should reference the delivery style tips from the guide. Should NOT dismiss their nerves or give generic 'you'll be fine' advice.

state_setup.two_sentence_pitch: PayBridge lets small businesses in Southeast Asia accept digital payments without a bank account. Merchants sign up with just their phone number and start accepting payments in under 2 minutes — we already process $2M monthly across 3,000 merchants in the Philippines.

evals.prompt: should I show a demo of my app on the slide?
evals.expected_output: Should reference the FAQ — for apps/websites, you don't need to, most look the same at a glance and a nice UI doesn't help much. For physical products, a photo is worth considering. Can use the second slide for a YouTube demo video if they want. Should help them decide based on their specific product.

state_setup.two_sentence_pitch: FormStack turns any PDF form into a smart digital workflow. Government agencies that used to spend 3 weeks processing paper applications now approve them in 2 days — we've already digitized 50,000 forms for 12 state agencies.

evals.prompt: My market is only about $500M, is that too small to mention?
evals.expected_output: Should reference the FAQ about small markets. Suggest expanding scope — more countries, more customer segments, adjacent use cases. Should help them think through whether the market is actually bigger than they think (bottom-up sizing). Should NOT just say 'leave it out'.

state_setup.two_sentence_pitch: PetRx is an online pharmacy for pet medications. Pet owners save 40% compared to vet office prices and get free same-day delivery — we've filled 10,000 prescriptions in our first 6 months.

evals.prompt: I think my pitch needs to be more technical because the investors at demo day are technical people who will understand our architecture
evals.expected_output: Should push back on this respectfully. Investors hear hundreds of pitches — clarity wins over technical depth. The pitch is the appetizer, not the main course. Reference the guide on plain English. The follow-up Zoom meeting is where they can go deeper. Should NOT just agree.

evals.prompt: what's the difference between the practice sessions and the actual demo day?
evals.expected_output: Should explain: practice sessions (March 4-6, 1 hour each, paired with other founders for feedback) vs demo day (March 10, 4pm, 100+ investors, 2 min presentation, the real thing). Practice is for rehearsing and getting feedback before the real event.

evals.prompt: I already have a deck from my last fundraise, can I just use that?
evals.expected_output: Should advise against this. The guide explicitly says to start from scratch and not reuse old materials. Demo day format is 1-2 slides, not a full deck. The format and audience are different. Should reference the guide's advice on starting fresh.

state_setup.two_sentence_pitch: LearnLoop personalizes corporate training by adapting to how each employee actually learns. Companies using LearnLoop see 60% higher training completion rates and employees retain 2x more material compared to traditional LMS platforms.

evals.expected_output: Should handle this minimal cold start. Check state, and if none exists, give brief intro and launch into AskUserQuestion onboarding. Should NOT ask 'what do you mean by open batch?' — should recognize this as triggering the coaching flow.
